title: LogPrinter
text: LogPrinter Specifies one or more logical printers for the application. Windows only. Syntax LogPrinter { = } PRT_ LogicalPrinterName Defaults Assignment file usys.ini Section [printer] Default value None Description A logical printer is a named collection of printer settings for a physical printer. You can define more than one logical printer to be mapped to the same physical printer. However, you cannot define multiple logical printers with the same name. To define logical printers, the Setup utility must be enabled for your application. For more information, see Setup Utility and Define a Logical Printer . The logical printer definitions are stored in the Windows Registry. For each logical printer, an entry is added to the LogPrinter setting in usys.ini . For example, if you defined a logical printer called DEF_LANDSCAPE , an entry called PRT_DEF_LANDSCAPE is added to LogPrinter : [printer] logprinter=PRT_DEF_PORTRAIT, PRT_DEF_LANDSCAPE Copy Search Path for Logical Printers When Uniface looks for logical printer definitions, it looks for logical printers under the following Registry keys in the specified order: HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Uniface/Uniface Version / ApsName HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Uniface/Uniface Version / HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Uniface/ UnifaceAdmDir / IniFileName HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/Software/Uniface/ UnifaceAdmDir / IniFileName HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/Software/Uniface/ UnifaceAdmDir /default_ini Version is the main version number, such as 9 or 10 . ApsName is the name of the application shell. UnifaceAdmDir is the directory specified by the USYSADM path logical. IniFileName is the name of an initialization file. If not specified, usys.ini is assumed. On Windows, Uniface always writes logical printer definitions to the second location. For logical printer definitions to be moved or copied to any other location, you must use $setting . Note: Due to Microsoft restrictions, Uniface writes to locations under HKEY_CURRENT_USER . To write to locations under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE , you must run Uniface as administrator and disable Registry Virtualization before using $setting . For more information, refer to Registry Virtualization on MSDN. CAUTION: Making changes to the Registry can significantly affect your computer, so use due care when using $setting . Uniface cannot be held responsible for changes you make to your Registry. Related Links Define a Logical Printer On Windows, you can define a logical printer, which is a named collection of printer settings for a physical printer. You can define more than one logical printer for the same physical printer. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
